name: Heitsi-Eibib
description: Heitsi-Eibib is a prominent and complex figure in Khoi mythology, occupying a space between a hero, a trickster, and a divine being. He is often described as an ancestral hero and magician, possessing supernatural powers and the ability to shapeshift. Heitsi-Eibib's origins are shrouded in mystery, but he is generally believed to have been born of a virgin woman, although different accounts vary. He is revered for his numerous exploits, including teaching the Khoi people essential skills like hunting, healing, and crafting. He is also known for his battles against evil monsters and his role in shaping the landscape. Heitsi-Eibib is associated with the creation of numerous landmarks, particularly piles of stones or mounds, which serve as his graves or resting places. These sites are considered sacred, and people often leave offerings or pray at them. His nature is ambivalent; he can be helpful and benevolent, but also mischievous and unpredictable. He is a powerful figure embodying both positive and negative aspects, reflecting the complexities of life itself. Heitsi-Eibib's legacy continues to be important in Khoi culture, representing resilience, adaptation, and the enduring power of ancestral wisdom. He is a cultural hero who embodies the values and beliefs of the Khoi people, serving as a reminder of their history and traditions. He is also considered to be immortal, dying and being reborn repeatedly.
subtitle: Hero, Trickster, and Divine Ancestor

keyMyths.title: Birth of Heitsi-Eibib
keyMyths.description: Heitsi-Eibib was born to a virgin woman, conceived through supernatural means. This miraculous birth marked him as a special being with extraordinary powers.
keyMyths.source: Bleek and Lloyd, Specimens of Bushman Folklore

keyMyths.title: Heitsi-Eibib and the Monster
keyMyths.description: Heitsi-Eibib fought and defeated a monstrous creature that threatened the Khoi people. This victory established him as a protector and hero.

keyMyths.title: Heitsi-Eibib's Graves
keyMyths.description: Heitsi-Eibib dies and is reborn in different locations, marked by piles of stones. These sites are considered sacred and are visited by people seeking blessings.

sources.author: W.H.I. Bleek and L.C. Lloyd
sources.description: A collection of stories and myths recorded from Khoisan informants.
sources.text: A collection of stories and myths recorded from Khoisan informants.

sources.author: J.M. Orpen
